arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; which dotnet 2>/dev/null || echo "no dotnet"

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try Microsoft symbol server - ELF buildid format
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
LIB="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try several known URL patterns for the MS symbol server
for pattern in \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${LIB}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${LIB}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${LIB}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${LIB}"; do
  echo "Trying: $pattern"
  code=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -L "$pattern")
  echo "  HTTP: $code"
  if [ "$code" = "200" ]; then
    echo "  FOUND!"
    curl -s -L -o /tmp/sv-15712f84af5d4da6bdc6df5adaa90bee/mono_debug_sym "$pattern"
    file /tmp/sv-15712f84af5d4da6bdc6df5adaa90bee/mono_debug_sym
    break
  fi
done
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this tombstone. The key frames are from `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. 

To symbolicate these, I would need:
1. The debug symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with that specific BuildId
2. A tool like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to resolve the addresses

The BuildId is `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. 

This is from the Mono runtime, which is what powers .NET on Android through MAUI. Without the actual debug symbols for this specific build, I can't fully symbolicate the frames—the BuildId identifies the exact build, but I'd need the corresponding debug information to map addresses to function names. Let me see what I can work with here.

Microsoft publishes debug symbols for .NET runtime libraries through their symbol server, so I could try accessing those using the BuildId. The typical approach would be to query the symbol server with the library name and BuildId, or potentially use the debuginfod protocol to fetch the symbols.

I don't have the dotnet tool readily available, so let me try fetching directly from Microsoft's symbol server using curl instead. The URL format for ELF symbols varies slightly depending on the naming convention, so I'll need to try a few different patterns to see which one works. I'll try checking what debugging tools are available on the system and attempt to fetch the symbols from Microsoft's symbol server.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; which dotnet 2>/dev/null || echo "no dotnet"

    echo "  FOUND!"
    curl -s -L -o /tmp/sv-15712f84af5d4da6bdc6df5adaa90bee/mono_debug_sym "$pattern"
    file /tmp/sv-15712f84af5d4da6bdc6df5adaa90bee/mono_debug_sym
    break
  fi
done

    file /tmp/sv-15712f84af5d4da6bdc6df5adaa90bee/mono_debug_sym

  fi
done

data.content: Got the debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol server. Now let me symbolicate the frames.

arguments.command:
SYMFILE="/tmp/sv-15712f84af5d4da6bdc6df5adaa90bee/mono_debug_sym"

# Addresses from the tombstone for libmonosgen-2.0.so frames
ADDRS="0x222098 0x2241ac 0x1abc80 0x1abd14 0x16dfa4 0x16db6c 0x16de04"

echo "=== Symbolicated .NET Runtime (Mono) Frames ==="
echo ""
for addr in $ADDRS; do
  result=$(addr2line -C -f -e "$SYMFILE" "$addr" 2>/dev/null)
  func=$(echo "$result" | head -1)
  loc=$(echo "$result" | tail -1)
  printf "  pc %-12s  %s\n                   at %s\n\n" "$addr" "$func" "$loc"
done

data.content:
Here's the symbolicated backtrace for the `libmonosgen-2.0.so` frames:

```
#00 pc 0x6ae1c   libc.so                (abort+164)
#01 pc 0x222098  libmonosgen-2.0.so     ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast
                                          at mono/metadata/icall.c:6244
#02 pc 0x2241ac  libmonosgen-2.0.so     ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast_raw
                                          at mono/metadata/icall-def.h:202
#03 pc 0x1abc80  libmonosgen-2.0.so     do_icall
                                          at mono/mini/interp/interp.c:2457
#04 pc 0x1abd14  libmonosgen-2.0.so     get_interp_to_native_trampoline
                                          at mono/mini/interp/interp.c:1732
#05 pc 0x16dfa4  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst
                                          at mono/mini/type-checking.c
#06 pc 0x16db6c  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst
                                          at mono/mini/type-checking.c:506
#07 pc 0x16de04  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst
                                          at mono/mini/type-checking.c:528
#08 pc 0x307c    <anonymous JIT code>
```

**Summary:** The crash is a `SIGABRT` triggered by `Environment.FailFast` (the abort message is `"not combatible"`). The call path goes through the Mono interpreter (`do_icall` → `interp_to_native_trampoline`) from a type-checking operation (`handle_isinst`), suggesting a compatibility check failed during a type cast at startup. Frame `#08` is JIT-compiled managed code which would require the app's managed symbols to resolve further.
